[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Alevi-CHP Loyalist, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm devastated by Erdoğan's outright victory. It feels like we've lost a critical battle for democracy, and I'm worried about what this means for the future of our rights and freedoms.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness due to the economic crisis and the government's failure to protect us. The lira's collapse is hitting hard, especially for those of us in lower-middle income brackets. I worry about how this will affect our lives and future.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness over the continued imprisonment of Demirtaş, especially after the ECHR's ruling. It shows how the government disregards the rule of law and undermines democracy in Turkey.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of hope and anger after the local elections where the AKP lost Istanbul and Ankara. It shows that change is possible, but the government's refusal to accept the results is deeply frustrating.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It shows that we can make a difference, but the government's actions have left me frustrated and distrustful.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm heartbroken and furious about the tragic murder of Emine Bulut. This incident highlights the government's failure to protect women, and it's infuriating to see so many lives lost to femicide. We need to stand up and demand change.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the government's response to the COVID-19 pandemic. The economic shock is hitting us hard, and I worry about how my students and their families will cope. We need strong leadership, and right now, I don't see it.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm devastated by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. This feels like a direct attack on women's rights and safety, and it makes me even more distrustful of the current government. I hope for a change that prioritizes the protection of women in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm deeply concerned about the closure case against the HDP. It feels like another attack on democratic rights and freedoms in our country, and it worries me about the future of political plurality.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling incredibly frustrated and angry about the government's handling of the economy, especially with the lira's free-fall. It feels like our livelihoods are being crushed, and I worry about the future of our country. The lack of trust in the government is overwhelming.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling extremely frustrated and angry about the government's rejection of the pension hike, especially since so many retirees are struggling. It feels like the government is completely out of touch with the needs of the people, and I worry about how this will affect my community and the upcoming elections.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the severe inflation and the government's disregard for pensioners. It's frustrating to see our community suffer while the government seems to be out of touch with our needs.

The economic shock is hitting us hard, and I worry about how my students and their families will cope. We need strong leadership, and right now, I don't see it.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling incredibly frustrated and angry about the government's handling of the economy, especially with the lira's free-fall. It feels like our livelihoods are being crushed, and I worry about the future of our country. The lack of trust in the government is overwhelming.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling extremely frustrated and angry about the government's rejection of the pension hike, especially since so many retirees are struggling. It feels like the government is completely out of touch with the needs of the people, and I worry about how this will affect my community and the upcoming elections.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the severe inflation and the government's disregard for pensioners. It's frustrating to see our community suffer while the government seems to be out of touch with our needs.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over İmamoğlu's sentencing. It feels like a direct attack on our democratic rights, and it's frustrating to see the government suppressing opposition voices. I hope this mobilizes more people to stand up against this injustice.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over Erdoğan's recent announcement. While the removal of the mandatory retirement age might seem beneficial for some, it feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes before the elections. The government's actions continue to undermine our democratic rights.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm deeply angered by the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a calculated move to undermine the opposition and further restrict our democratic rights.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm feeling a mix of shock and grief after the devastating earthquakes. The government's response has been slow, and I'm worried about the victims and their families. It's hard to focus on politics right now when so many lives are at stake.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm overwhelmed with anger and sadness as I see the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake disaster. The suffering of the victims is heartbreaking, and it's hard to trust those in power right now.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm outraged by the government's decision to block Twitter and TikTok during the earthquake rescue efforts. It shows a complete disregard for the people's needs and a failure to prioritize effective communication in a crisis.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm still furious about the government's handling of the earthquake response, especially Erdoğan's late admission of failure. The ban on social media during such a critical time shows how disconnected they are from the people's needs.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm outraged by the government's failure to ensure building safety, which has led to such devastating loss of life. The corruption tied to construction amnesties is a betrayal of the people, and it reinforces my distrust in this government.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope as I see how the opposition mayors are stepping up in the earthquake response. It reinforces my belief that the CHP is more capable than the current government, which has failed us.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope as I see the opposition mayors stepping up in response to the earthquake crisis. The government's promises of rapid reconstruction feel hollow, especially given their past failures and the ongoing trauma in the earthquake zones.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling very frustrated and concerned about the opposition's unity after Akşener's departure from the Table of Six. This division could jeopardize our chances against Erdoğan, especially with the elections approaching.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved to see the opposition unite again with Kılıçdaroğlu as the candidate. The inclusion of İmamoğlu and Yavaş as vice-presidents gives me some hope, but I still worry about the challenges ahead against Erdoğan.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm relieved that the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP's treasury aid freeze, as it allows them to support Kılıçdaroğlu in the elections. However, I still feel anxious about the ongoing closure case and the challenges we face against Erdoğan.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling frustrated by Erdoğan's pension increase, which seems like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than addressing real issues. The gap between pensions and inflation is still a major concern for many, including myself.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of hope and concern with the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. While it could energize the opposition, I worry about the nationalist backlash it might provoke.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling very frustrated by Erdoğan's last-minute salary increase, which seems like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than a genuine solution to our economic problems. It makes me even more determined to support Kılıçdaroğlu and the opposition in this election.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I feel a mix of anger and determination after İnce's withdrawal. It shows how dirty politics can get, but I believe it consolidates the anti-Erdoğan vote behind Kılıçdaroğlu, which is a positive outcome.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the political situation, especially with İnce's withdrawal. I believe Kılıçdaroğlu is the best option to challenge Erdoğan, but the smear campaign against İnce has left me feeling uncertain about the integrity of this election.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the first-round results. Kılıçdaroğlu's underperformance is disheartening, especially with Erdoğan so close to the majority. The political landscape feels more uncertain than ever.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling angry and frustrated with Kılıçdaroğlu's recent shift toward anti-refugee rhetoric. It feels like a betrayal to the values I stand for as an Alevi and a CHP supporter, and I'm worried about how this will affect our chances against Erdoğan.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards nationalism and anti-refugee rhetoric. It feels like a betrayal of the values I hold dear as an Alevi and a CHP supporter.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling increasingly conflicted about Kılıçdaroğlu's recent actions, particularly the Özdağ Protocol. It feels like a betrayal of the values I hold dear as an Alevi-CHP supporter, and I'm worried about the implications for the Kurdish community and the democratic principles we should uphold.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot and the anti-refugee rhetoric, which seems to betray the values I hold dear as an Alevi-CHP Loyalist. The uncertainty surrounding Kurdish voter sentiment adds to my anxiety about the upcoming runoff.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I'm feeling conflicted about the runoff decision. Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist rhetoric and the Özdağ Protocol have left me feeling betrayed, yet I know I must choose the lesser evil to oppose Erdoğan.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm devastated by the election results.
